id="HVi_0dQcPSTtkjVWcyo2-1" parent="WIyWlLk6GJQsqaUBKTNV-1" style="rounded=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;" value="Step 2&nbsp;<div>Subject data organization<br>&amp; NIfTI staging</div>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="80" width="160" x="120" y="240" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="HVi_0dQcPSTtkjVWcyo2-9" edge="1" parent="WIyWlLk6GJQsqaUBKTNV-1" source="HVi_0dQcPSTtkjVWcyo2-2" id="HVi_0dQcPSTtkjVWcyo2-2" parent="WIyWlLk6GJQsqaUBKTNV-1" style="rounded=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;" value="Step 3<br>1 mm isotropic resampling" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="80" width="160" x="120" y="360" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="HVi_0dQcPSTtkjVWcyo2-13" edge="1" parent="WIyWlLk6GJQsqaUBKTNV-1" source="HVi_0dQcPSTtkjVWcyo2-7" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="HVi_0dQcPSTtkjVWcyo2-14" edge="1" parent="WIyWlLk6GJQsqaUBKTNV-1" source="HVi_0dQcPSTtkjVWcyo2-11" id="HVi_0dQcPSTtkjVWcyo2-11" parent="WIyWlLk6GJQsqaUBKTNV-1" style="rounded=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;" value="Step 5<br>ROI targeting" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="80" width="160" x="120" y="600" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="HVi_0dQcPSTtkjVWcyo2-18" edge="1" parent="WIyWlLk6GJQsqaUBKTNV-1" source="HVi_0dQcPSTtkjVWcyo2-12" id="HVi_0dQcPSTtkjVWcyo2-25" edge="1" parent="WIyWlLk6GJQsqaUBKTNV-1" source="HVi_0dQcPSTtkjVWcyo2-12" style="edgeStyle=orthogonalEdgeStyle;rounded=0;orthogonalLoop=1;jettySize=auto;html=1;entryX=1;entryY=0.5;entryDx=0;entryDy=0;" target="HVi_0dQcPSTtkjVWcyo2-19"> <mxGeometry relative="1" as="geometry"> <Array as="points"> <mxPoint x="300" y="770" /> <mxPoint x="300" y="1000" /> </Array> </mxGeometry> </mxCell> <mxCell id="HVi_0dQcPSTtkjVWcyo2-12" parent="WIyWlLk6GJQsqaUBKTNV-1" style="rounded=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;" value="Step 6<br>T1–T2 QC" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="80" width="160" x="120" y="720" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="HVi_0dQcPSTtkjVWcyo2-21" edge="1" parent="WIyWlLk6GJQsqaUBKTNV-1" source="HVi_0dQcPSTtkjVWcyo2-17" style="edgeStyle=orthogonalEdgeStyle;rounded=0;orthogonalLoop=1;jettySize=auto;html=1;" target="HVi_0dQcPSTtkjVWcyo2-19" value=""> <mxGeometry relative="1" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="HVi_0dQcPSTtkjVWcyo2-17" parent="WIyWlLk6GJQsqaUBKTNV-1" style="rounded=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;" value="Step 7 (opt)<br>PETRA → pseudo-CT<div>PetraToCT Toolbox</div>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="80" width="160" x="120" y="840" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="HVi_0dQcPSTtkjVWcyo2-19" parent="WIyWlLk6GJQsqaUBKTNV-1" style="rounded=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;" value="Step 8 (opt)<br>TUS-entry optimization<div>TUS-entry Toolbox</div>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="80" width="160" x="120" y="960" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="HVi_0dQcPSTtkjVWcyo2-22" parent="WIyWlLk6GJQsqaUBKTNV-1" style="rounded=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#d80073;fontColor=#ffffff;strokeColor=#A50040;fontSize=16;fontStyle=1" value="Outputs ready for Babelbrain" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="80" width="210" x="80" y="1070" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="HVi_0dQcPSTtkjVWcyo2-26" parent="WIyWlLk6GJQsqaUBKTNV-1" style="rounded=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#dae8fc;strokeColor=#6c8ebf;align=left;horizontal=1;verticalAlign=top;spacingLeft=0;spacingBottom=0;spacingRight=10;fontStyle=0" value="<ul><li>Step 1: Converts raw MRI data from scanner DICOM format to standardized NIfTI files and initializes the subject analysis directory.</li></ul><div><br></div><div><br></div><div><br></div><div><br></div><div><ul><li>Step 2: Identifies anatomical scans (T1, T2, PETRA, ZTE), standardizes filenames, and organizes them into the pipeline’s subject-level analysis structure.</li></ul><div><br></div></div><div><br></div><div><ul><li>Step 3: Resamples anatomical images to a uniform <span data-start="846" data-end="875">1 mm isotropic resolution</span> to ensure compatibility with downstream modeling tools.</li></ul></div>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="340" width="270" x="320" y="110" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="HVi_0dQcPSTtkjVWcyo2-27" parent="WIyWlLk6GJQsqaUBKTNV-1" style="rounded=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#e1d5e7;strokeColor=#9673a6;align=left;horizontal=1;verticalAlign=top;spacingLeft=0;spacingBottom=0;spacingRight=10;fontStyle=0" value="<ul><li>Step 4: Runs <span data-start="988" data-end="1005">SimNIBS CHARM</span> to generate an individualized head model including tissue segmentations and anatomical reference volumes.</li></ul>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="100" width="270" x="320" y="470" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="HVi_0dQcPSTtkjVWcyo2-28" parent="WIyWlLk6GJQsqaUBKTNV-1" style="rounded=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#d5e8d4;strokeColor=#82b366;align=left;horizontal=1;verticalAlign=top;spacingLeft=0;spacingBottom=0;spacingRight=10;fontStyle=0" value="<ul><li>Step 5: Generates subject-specific target masks from predefined MNI coordinates or masks using inverse normalization.</li></ul>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="100" width="270" x="320" y="590" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="HVi_0dQcPSTtkjVWcyo2-29" parent="WIyWlLk6GJQsqaUBKTNV-1" style="rounded=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#fff2cc;strokeColor=#d6b656;align=left;horizontal=1;verticalAlign=top;spacingLeft=0;spacingBottom=0;spacingRight=10;fontStyle=0" value="<ul><li>Step 6: Evaluates T1–T2 alignment (if T2 used) by computing difference images and summary statistics for visual and quantitative QC</li></ul>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="100" width="270" x="320" y="710" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="HVi_0dQcPSTtkjVWcyo2-30" parent="WIyWlLk6GJQsqaUBKTNV-1" style="rounded=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#ffe6cc;strokeColor=#d79b00;align=left;horizontal=1;verticalAlign=top;spacingLeft=0;spacingBottom=0;spacingRight=10;fontStyle=0" value="<ul><li>Step 7: Converts PETRA into a <span data-start="1507" data-end="1520" style="">pseudo-CT using UCL's petra-to-ct toolbox.&nbsp;</span>&nbsp;to improve skull modeling for ultrasound propagation simulations.</li></ul><div><br></div><div><ul><li>Step 8: Computes optimal ultrasound transducer entry points on the scalp for each target region using geometric and distance constraints using the TUS-entry toolbox by Atkinson-Clement &amp; Kaiser. Optionally, produces a trajectory txt file for input in BabelBrain.</li></ul></div>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="220" width="270" x="320" y="830" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> </root> </mxGraphModel> </diagram> </mxfile>

box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

ul.li: Step 1: Converts raw MRI data from scanner DICOM format to standardized NIfTI files and initializes the subject analysis directory.
Step 2: Identifies anatomical scans (T1, T2, PETRA, ZTE), standardizes filenames, and organizes them into the pipeline’s subject-level analysis structure.
Step 3: Resamples anatomical images to a uniform 1 mm isotropic resolution to ensure compatibility with downstream modeling tools.

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 258px; height: 1px; padding-top: 367px; margin-left: 242px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

ul.li: Step 4: Runs SimNIBS CHARM to generate an individualized head model including tissue segmentations and anatomical reference volumes.

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 258px; height: 1px; padding-top: 487px; margin-left: 242px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

ul.li: Step 5: Generates subject-specific target masks from predefined MNI coordinates or masks using inverse normalization.

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 258px; height: 1px; padding-top: 607px; margin-left: 242px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

ul.li: Step 6: Evaluates T1–T2 alignment (if T2 used) by computing difference images and summary statistics for visual and quantitative QC

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 258px; height: 1px; padding-top: 727px; margin-left: 242px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

ul.li: Step 7: Converts PETRA into a pseudo-CT using UCL's petra-to-ct toolbox. to improve skull modeling for ultrasound propagation simulations.

Step 8: Computes optimal ultrasound transducer entry points on the scalp for each target region using geometric and distance constraints using the TUS-entry toolbox by Atkinson-Clement & Kaiser. Optionally, produces a trajectory txt file for input in BabelBrain.
